articles.title: Fed rate cuts pushed to late 2026 as Middle East war fuels inflation surge

articles.title: Japan PMI shows manufacturing surge as services slowdown caps growth
articles.expand: Japan’s composite PMI eased to 52.4 (exp. 51.4) as manufacturing surged to 54.9 but services slowed to 51.2. Strong factory output offset weaker domestic demand, while rising costs and falling confidence point to a more uneven growth outlook. articles.title: Gold drifts lower below $4,750 amid Trump’s extension of a ceasefire with Iran
articles.expand: Gold price (XAU/USD) trades in negative territory near $4,730 during the early Asian session on Thursday. The precious metal declines as traders remain cautious over US-Iran peace talks to end the nearly two-month war that has intensified global inflation concerns.

articles.title: USD/JPY steadies ahead of Japan CPI and US PMIs
articles.expand: USD/JPY was little changed on Wednesday, hovering close to 159.50 in a narrow session after Tuesday's push to 159.64. Price has been confined between 159.10 and 159.60 through the midweek stretch, with overlapping small-bodied candles pointing to indecision.
